title: Rivian Q2 2023 Earnings Report
text: Rivian Automotive exceeded Wall Street expectations in its second-quarter 2023 results. The company generated revenue of $1.12 billion, surpassing analyst estimates of $1 billion. On an adjusted basis, the firm reported a per-share loss of $1.08, narrower than the projected loss of $1.41. Management raised its annual production forecast to 52,000 vehicles for 2023, up from the previous guidance of 50,000 units—representing a 114% increase compared to 2022's output of 24,337 vehicles. The company produced 13,992 vehicles during the quarter, a 49% increase from Q1. Vehicle deliveries reached 12,640 units, up 59% sequentially. The operation generated $1.12 billion in revenue, with regulatory credit sales contributing $34 million. Despite revenue gains, Rivian continues facing significant cash burn challenges. The firm used $1.36 billion in operational cash during the quarter, resulting in negative free cash flow of $1.62 billion. The company ended the period with $10.2 billion in liquid assets, providing approximately 1.5 years of runway at current burn rates. Amazon's 100,000-unit electric delivery van order continues progressing, with vehicles now operating across more than 800 U.S. cities.
